jokes.text: There's pictures in there. Oh! There are pictures. There are lots of pictures.
jokes.setup: Ben warns Leslie not to open folder of senior sex-ed thank you notes because there are pictures
jokes.punchline: Leslie opens it anyway and is horrified by what she sees - implied nude photos from seniors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from warning to horror, relying on audience imagination for the cringe. Well-structured but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Because every memory deserves to be chronicled. Even the saggy ones.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks why Ben wouldn't throw away inappropriate senior photos
jokes.punchline: Ben's earnest response about chronicling memories with 'saggy ones' working as both emotional memories and literal physical description
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning delivered with Ben's characteristic earnestness. Perfect character integration with his scrapbooking obsession.

jokes.text: Probably would've got more stuff done if I hadn't spent so much time brainstorming scrapbook titles.
jokes.setup: Ben laments not accomplishing more in his first year on city council
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was distracted by the very scrapbooking he's doing to commemorate his accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben Wyatt neurotic perfectionism - being unproductive because he's obsessing over documenting productivity.

jokes.text: But what if we took a day on and stayed here and worked on some projects? That sounds pretty sexy, right?
jokes.setup: Ben suggests they take a day off to relax
jokes.punchline: Leslie counters by suggesting they work instead and calls it 'sexy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie Knope - finding government work sexually appealing. Pure character comedy that defines her workaholic nature.

jokes.text: We are all out of water, actually. I'll have to use boiling hot milk. Are you really gonna do that? Yes.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April to water her plants while she's gone
jokes.punchline: April deadpans that she'll use boiling hot milk instead of water, confirming she'll actually do this plant-killing act
jokes.explanation: Perfect April Ludgate deadpan destruction. The absurdity of boiling milk to kill plants delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: What the fuck is happening right now?
jokes.setup: Jerry announces it's his last day and he's retiring
jokes.punchline: Leslie's complete bewilderment at Jerry's retirement announcement
jokes.explanation: Raw reaction that captures Leslie's shock. Simple but effective use of profanity for emphasis.

jokes.text: Well, I didn't want to make a big fuss. Although, gosh, I'm pretty sure I mentioned it a few times.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains why he didn't tell them about retiring
jokes.punchline: Jerry realizes he actually did mention it multiple times, undermining his own explanation
jokes.explanation: Jerry's characteristic bumbling self-awareness. Shows how ignored he is that his retirement announcements went unnoticed.

jokes.text: Can you believe it? Only six months left until I retire. / What did Jerry say? / Jerry was here?
jokes.setup: Flashback to Jerry previously announcing his retirement
jokes.punchline: Tom and Ron's complete obliviousness to Jerry's presence even when he's speaking directly to them
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of the Jerry-is-invisible running gag. The editing structure enhances the comedy.

jokes.text: Well, this is my last weep. Oops. I mean, 'weep.' Week. [Laughs] Jeez.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to say 'week'
jokes.punchline: Jerry's multiple mispronunciations getting progressively more flustered
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry bumbling speech pattern. Functional character comedy but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: As much as we want this to happen, we can't let Jerry just disappear.
jokes.setup: Leslie deciding they need to commemorate Jerry's retirement
jokes.punchline: Leslie acknowledges everyone wants Jerry gone while simultaneously being the only one who cares about honoring him
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic inability to be mean even when acknowledging others want to be. Good character work.

jokes.text: Jerry, thank you for your service. Good-bye. Thanks, Ron. That speech means a lot.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to commemorate Jerry somehow
jokes.punchline: Ron's hilariously brief 'speech' and Jerry's genuine gratitude for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson minimalism contrasted with Jerry's pathetic gratitude. Maximum comedy from minimal words.

jokes.text: Okay, see you tomorrow, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry is retiring and saying goodbye
jokes.punchline: Andy completely missing the point and expecting to see Jerry tomorrow
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy obliviousness. Simple but effective character comedy showing he wasn't listening.

jokes.text: You have the resting heart rate of a 100-year-old tortoise.
jokes.setup: Doctor telling Chris he's extremely healthy
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison to a century-old turtle's heart rate as a positive health indicator
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd metaphor for extreme health. The tortoise comparison is unexpected and vivid.

jokes.text: Today? Like, today, today? This today?
jokes.setup: Doctor suggests Chris provide sperm sample today
jokes.punchline: Chris's mounting panic expressed through repetitive questioning of 'today'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Chris's anxiety through speech patterns. The repetition builds nervous energy effectively.

jokes.text: a scathing performance review. I mean, why would anyone be so mean? Oh, wait. I wrote this. I stand by it.
jokes.setup: Leslie reading Jerry's file and finding a mean performance review
jokes.punchline: Leslie's outrage at the cruelty followed by realizing she wrote it and immediately defending it
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Leslie character work - her principles immediately override her sympathy when she realizes it was her decision.

jokes.text: Well, Jerry was never really the type to rise above mediocrity or to it.
jokes.setup: Ben seemingly defending Jerry's lack of achievement
jokes.punchline: The addition of 'or to it' suggesting Jerry never even reached mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed insult with perfect economy. The delayed punchline makes Jerry's incompetence even more devastating.

jokes.text: Mac and cheese pizza? You're making that?
jokes.setup: Ben mentions making mac and cheese pizza
jokes.punchline: Leslie's shocked reaction to this culinary abomination
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's strange culinary choices

jokes.text: Torturing Jerry was my favorite thing in the world. After making out with you.
jokes.setup: April expressing sadness about Jerry leaving
jokes.punchline: April ranking Jerry torture as her second favorite activity after romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - combining cruelty with unexpected romantic sentiment. The ranking system is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Every place I've ever worked in has had a 'Jerry.' When one Jerry leaves, the office naturally selects a new Jerry to fill that role. It's Social Darwinism.
jokes.setup: Andy wondering who they'll make fun of without Jerry
jokes.punchline: Tom's pseudo-scientific explanation of office scapegoat dynamics using evolutionary terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on sitcom dynamics disguised as character dialogue. The scientific framing makes the cruelty darkly funny.

jokes.text: Hello, Ann Perkins, my fallopian princess.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann's work relationship ending so they can have a baby
jokes.punchline: Chris's bizarrely medical yet romantic pet name combining anatomy with royalty
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Chris Traeger - combining his fitness obsession with romantic awkwardness. 'Fallopian princess' is memorably weird.

jokes.text: Make a deposit in here. If you fill it up all the way, you get on the board.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining the sperm donation process to Chris
jokes.punchline: Ann's matter-of-fact explanation accidentally sounding like a game or achievement system
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo humor. The 'get on the board' phrasing makes clinical procedure sound like a contest.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. I go in alone with this pornographic disc and do it myself. And you stay out here and try not to think about what I'm doing in there.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining the sperm donation logistics
jokes.punchline: Chris's clinical yet mortifying explanation of the masturbation process
jokes.explanation: Awkward situation played for cringe. Chris's overly detailed explanation makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I panicked. Run!
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann dealing with awkwardness of fertility clinic
jokes.punchline: Chris's complete breakdown and flight response to the clinical baby-making process
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Chris's characteristic overthinking leading to panic. The brevity makes it punchy.

jokes.text: I made two pile files. [Laughter] Pilo fibles. Ugh. Filo pilos. Filo pilos? That's not right. Are you broken?
jokes.setup: Gareth trying to say 'file piles' correctly
jokes.punchline: Gareth's repeated mispronunciations getting worse each time, culminating in April's deadpan 'Are you broken?'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating speech malfunction comedy. April's response is perfectly deadpan. Sets up Gareth as potential new Jerry.

jokes.text: Well, I guess that settles who the new Jerry is. No, it doesn't! I barely even said it wrong.
jokes.setup: Tom identifying Gareth as the new Jerry after his speech malfunction
jokes.punchline: Gareth's immediate panic and denial about becoming the office scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Jerry replacement theory. Gareth's immediate terror at the designation is relatable.

jokes.text: One screwed-up sentence, and 30 years later, I'm wearing aquamarine sweater vests and listening to Bonnie Raitt and The da Vinci Code on my iPod.
jokes.setup: Gareth panicking about becoming the new Jerry
jokes.punchline: Gareth's vivid and oddly specific vision of his future Jerry-like existence
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific details about Jerry-like decline. The combination of fashion and entertainment choices is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: City Councilor Leslie Knope, clearance level four. Guests are only allowed on Wednesdays. And we don't have clearance levels.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to use official authority to get Jerry into private dining room
jokes.punchline: Security guard's matter-of-fact deflation of Leslie's made-up authority system
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Leslie's bureaucratic overreach. The guard's deadpan correction is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I put my arm around nothing. And then you Photoshop him in later, okay?
jokes.setup: Leslie determined to get Jerry a photo in the private dining room despite being denied entry
jokes.punchline: Leslie's convoluted plan to fake the photo by posing with empty air
jokes.explanation: Peak Leslie determination leading to absurd solution. Her commitment to Jerry's happiness drives the comedy.

jokes.text: Sudoku is easy. Is there even rules to this game?
jokes.setup: Andy dismissively claiming sudoku is easy
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately revealing he doesn't understand the basic concept of the game having rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy Dwyer logic - confidence immediately undermined by cluelessness. Great setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm like a giant squid. Hey, honey, I'm ink Dracula. Squid Dracula is going to get you.
jokes.setup: Andy gets ink all over himself
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiastic embrace of his ink-covered state through increasingly absurd personas
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy turning mishap into imaginative play. 'Squid Dracula' is wonderfully absurd character creation.

jokes.text: Andy will never be the new Jerry. Nothing embarrasses him. He's like a giant puppy with no shame.
jokes.setup: Gareth hoping Andy will become the new Jerry
jokes.punchline: April's astute observation that Andy's shamelessness makes him immune to Jerry treatment
jokes.explanation: Insightful character analysis delivered as joke. The 'giant puppy' comparison perfectly captures Andy's nature.

jokes.text: He resigned because of corruption and racketeering, and then he died from an overdose of pills. But actually his real cause of death was being thrown out of a helicopter while handcuffed.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Mayor Knudson's background to Jerry
jokes.punchline: Leslie's casual recounting of increasingly dramatic and violent death circumstances
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of mayor's demise. The matter-of-fact delivery makes the absurd violence funnier.

jokes.text: Some people say he's still alive because they never found the body, but they never found the body because he exploded on impact.
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing the mayor's death story
jokes.punchline: Leslie's logical explanation for why conspiracy theorists are wrong - he literally exploded
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical conclusion to absurd premise. The structure sets up conspiracy theory only to destroy it with even more violence.

jokes.text: First of all, I'm so sorry that those Serbian mobsters shoved you out of that helicopter.
jokes.setup: Jerry meeting the dead mayor's statue
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sincere condolences to a statue about specific violent death details
jokes.explanation: Jerry's earnest politeness to a statue about absurd death circumstances. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Ronfire of the vanities.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to create a nickname for Ron
jokes.punchline: Tom's terrible pun combining Ron's name with 'Bonfire of the Vanities'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's perfectly in character for Tom's tendency toward bad wordplay. The badness is the point.

jokes.text: Interns cost nothing to the taxpayer. And more importantly, they're usually stupid and terrible. So they get no work done. It's like a Jerry you don't have to pay.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why Ron should hire an intern
jokes.punchline: Tom's cynical reasoning that useless interns are perfect because they sabotage government efficiency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical intern pitch. Tom's anti-government efficiency argument would appeal to Ron's libertarian views.

jokes.text: Let me remove my clothes so we may begin to engage in physical activity to multiply our genes.
jokes.setup: Chris imagining if sex were as clinical as fertility treatment
jokes.punchline: Chris's robotic interpretation of romantic intimacy using scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger fitness-obsessed clinical approach to intimacy. The formal language makes it absurdly unsexy.

jokes.text: She has a tasty surprise waiting for me, if you know what I mean. Gross, Jerry. She made a meatloaf.
jokes.setup: Jerry's suggestive comment about Gayle's 'tasty surprise'
jokes.punchline: The 'surprise' is literally just meatloaf, making Leslie's disgust about food rather than sex
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection playing on audience expectations. Jerry's innocence and Leslie's food aversion create perfect reversal.

jokes.text: Jerry Gergich Memorial Conference Room. R.I.P.? Oh, the guy at the plaque store thought you had died. It was a rush job, so we didn't have time to change it.
jokes.setup: Leslie presenting Jerry with a conference room named after him
jokes.punchline: The plaque accidentally treats Jerry as deceased due to miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment. The memorial plaque mistake is believable and awkward, enhanced by Leslie's casual explanation.

jokes.text: It doesn't matter. We can just pretend it means retire in peace.
jokes.setup: Jerry receiving a memorial plaque while still alive
jokes.punchline: Jerry's gracious acceptance and attempt to find positive meaning in his premature memorialization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry response - finding contentment in even the most awkward situations. His optimism makes it both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Uh, you're on fire. [Gasps] Oh! Oh, my God, Jerry!
jokes.setup: Jerry giving a retirement speech at his cake ceremony
jokes.punchline: Jerry literally catches fire during his speech, creating chaos
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry disaster. Physical comedy escalation from peaceful ceremony to literal fire. High impact visual gag.

jokes.text: Well, that went roughly as well as I thought it would.
jokes.setup: Jerry's retirement ceremony ending with him on fire
jokes.punchline: Leslie's understated reaction to complete disaster, suggesting she expected this outcome
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to chaos. Leslie's acceptance that Jerry events always go wrong shows character development.

jokes.text: See you tomorrow, Jeremy. Well, pretty heroic, huh, guys? Hard to make fun of that.
jokes.setup: Jerry leaving after being set on fire during retirement ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ron calling Jerry heroic for surviving his own retirement party, April noting this makes mockery difficult
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment where even Ron finds grudging respect for Jerry's disaster survival. April's practical concern is funny.

jokes.text: Filo Pilo ruined the cake. Classic Pilo. What how is that my fault? Stupid Jerry. Should have never saved his life.
jokes.setup: The cake being ruined during Jerry's fire incident
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately blaming the new Jerry (Gareth) and Gareth's frustrated response blaming actual Jerry
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Jerry replacement dynamic. Gareth's exasperation at being blamed for everything is relatable.

jokes.text: Really? Your purse is shaped like a waffle. Please join us. The Gergiches don't take no for an answer.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she's not into breakfast foods
jokes.punchline: Gayle's sharp observation about Leslie's waffle purse contradicting her claim, followed by insistent hospitality
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy. Gayle's attention to detail undermines Leslie's protest perfectly. Good character insight.

jokes.text: Actually, I quit football senior year so I could help my pastor run a homeless shelter. Oh. Pfftt! This guy loves the homeless so much, he should marry them, right?
jokes.setup: Taylor revealing his noble volunteer work background
jokes.punchline: Tom's weak attempt to mock someone who's genuinely admirable, falling flat
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tom's inability to find anything mockable about Taylor. The failure of the insult is the joke.

jokes.text: This guy loves the homeless so much, he should marry them, right?
jokes.setup: Taylor reveals his charity work
jokes.punchline: Tom's attempt to mock him falls flat because the behavior is admirable
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's failed attempt to find something mockable about the perfect intern

jokes.text: Wish I could, man, but I'm not a 'marriage' dude. I've got a lot of love to give, and it just seems unfair to put boundaries on it.
jokes.setup: Andy jokingly asking Taylor to marry him and April
jokes.punchline: Taylor's earnest philosophical response about unlimited love to a clearly joking marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Taylor's over-sincere response to obvious joke. His new-age philosophy makes him unmockable in a different way than Andy.

jokes.text: Yeah, I could stop, and you could walk. Or you know what, we could meet in the middle. Great plan.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann awkwardly encountering each other after fertility clinic incident
jokes.punchline: Their overly formal negotiation of basic walking logistics showing post-intimacy awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good awkward post-hookup energy. The formality of their interaction shows how they're overcompensating for intimacy.

jokes.text: I already was Jerry. I was a skinny Indian kid in South Carolina, and it sucked. It took me 12 years, but I reinvented myself. I'm a business owner. I wear dope suits. I have fur underwear.
jokes.setup: Tom panicking about becoming the new Jerry
jokes.punchline: Tom's vulnerable admission of past bullying followed by increasingly absurd claims of current success
jokes.explanation: Excellent character depth moment. Tom's genuine vulnerability mixed with ridiculous status claims. 'Fur underwear' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: ♪ Eggs, bacon, and toast ♪ ♪ Why don't you start ♪ ♪ your day ♪ ♪ The Gergich way with eggs, ♪ ♪ bacon, and toast ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie visiting Jerry's home for breakfast
jokes.punchline: The Gergich family's elaborate morning song routine that they perform daily
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Jerry's family's cheerful weirdness. The daily song routine is perfectly awkward for outsiders.

jokes.text: You are still the most handsome man in the world to me. Ugh. Mom, come on. Yeah, jeez, mom. We're eating.
jokes.setup: Gayle praising Jerry's appearance despite his obvious unattractiveness
jokes.punchline: The daughters' disgusted reaction to their parents' romantic display
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic comedy. The daughters' reaction to parental romance is universally relatable and well-timed.

jokes.text: for me, the best part about working in the Parks Department was that I got to be home every night with my family at 5:00. And to me, that's what mattered most.
jokes.setup: Jerry admitting he didn't achieve his work goals
jokes.punchline: Jerry finding genuine satisfaction in work-life balance rather than career achievement
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment that reframes Jerry's 'failure' as success. The irony works both comedically and emotionally.

jokes.text: [Imitates revving motor] The hug machine--it's firing on all cylinders.
jokes.setup: Jerry's family initiating a group hug
jokes.punchline: Jerry's enthusiastic mechanical metaphor for family affection complete with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Endearing Jerry dad-joke energy. The mechanical metaphor for family love is sweetly dorky and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Do you want to make out? I really do.
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann having serious discussion about co-parenting
jokes.punchline: Chris's abrupt shift from mature relationship talk to immediate physical desire
jokes.explanation: Good emotional honesty moment. Chris's directness cuts through relationship complexity with simple desire. Very economical.

jokes.text: Jerry will come in once a week, and everyone will get to watch him eat and talk to him about anything he might do or say or fart.
jokes.setup: Ron explains Jerry's part-time return
jokes.punchline: He frames it as entertainment for the staff to mock Jerry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - brutally honest about using Jerry for entertainment

jokes.text: Classic new guy.
jokes.setup: Jerry immediately trips when returning to work
jokes.punchline: Tom ironically calling Jerry the 'new guy' despite him working there for 40+ years
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic callback. Jerry's immediate return to form (tripping) reinforces his eternal Jerry-ness regardless of status.

jokes.text: As I suspected--airtight.
jokes.setup: Ben mentioning he checked Fringe for plot holes
jokes.punchline: Ben's satisfied confirmation that the show has no logical inconsistencies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerdiness. His satisfaction with fictional logical consistency is both endearing and ridiculous.

jokes.text: Like, was she a Russian spy and the KGB forced her to marry Jerry as a cover? You know, it's a mystery, Ben. Let it go.
jokes.setup: Ben's ongoing confusion about Jerry's attractive wife
jokes.punchline: Ben's elaborate spy conspiracy theory to explain Gayle's attraction to Jerry
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd theory that fits Ben's analytical mind. The specificity of KGB involvement makes it memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Hey, maybe Gayle has one of those Oliver Sacks brain disorder thingies? Like, does she think Jerry is a friendly hat?
jokes.setup: Ben continuing to theorize about Jerry and Gayle's relationship
jokes.punchline: Ben's reference to neurological disorders where Gayle might literally see Jerry as an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist theory combining Ben's intelligence with absurd conclusion. 'Friendly hat' is perfectly specific and ridiculous.
